model_narrative: We run a farm that chooses how to allocate its land across several crops, each of which can be grown under different cropping schedules and at different irrigation levels. We also decide how much of each field task to carry out with each combination of implement and power source in each fortnight of the year, how much equipment to buy, how much labor to hire in each fortnight, and how much of each crop to sell. The goal is to make the farm's profit as large as possible, where profit is the revenue from crop sales minus the total of all the farm's costs.

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the crop commodities the farm can grow

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the alternative cropping schedules, meaning the calendar plans for planting and growing a crop

sets.name: w
sets.doc: the irrigation or water-stress levels along a crop's yield-water response curve, from fully watered to increasingly water-stressed

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the time periods of the year, given as fortnights, numbered 1 through 24

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the agricultural field tasks, such as ploughing, spraying, drilling, harvesting and transport

sets.name: mn
sets.doc: every equipment item, covering both the implements that do field work and the power sources that drive them

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the implements, meaning the tools and attachments that perform a task; a subset of the equipment items

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the power sources that drive the implements, such as tractors and draft animals; a subset of the equipment items

sets.name: cc
sets.doc: the cost classifications the farm's total cost is broken into

sets.name: cposs
sets.doc: the crop and schedule combinations that are agronomically feasible; only these crop-schedule pairs may be planted

sets.name: taskposs
sets.doc: the task and time pairs at which a task is allowed to occur; only these task-time combinations are active

sets.name: equipposs
sets.doc: the equipment and time pairs at which an equipment item may be used; an equipment-balance requirement applies only on these pairs

sets.name: xwaterset
sets.doc: the crop and water-stress pairs that lie on a crop's yield-water response curve; the irrigated-area variable and the yield and water coefficients are defined only on these pairs

sets.name: taskset
sets.index: subset of g x t x m x n
sets.doc: the task, time, implement and power tuples that are structurally active, meaning the technology or operating-cost coefficient is nonzero; the task-activity variable is defined only on these tuples

sets.name: equippset
sets.doc: the equipment items that carry a capital cost or an equipment-balance requirement; the equipment-purchase variable is defined only on these items

params.name: oc
params.doc: operating cost in 1000 rials per hectare for performing a task with a given implement and power source

params.name: avail
params.doc: the availability of each equipment item, in working hours per fortnight per unit owned

params.name: cap
params.doc: the amortized capital cost in 1000 rials for owning one unit of an equipment item

params.name: pmisc
params.doc: the cost of miscellaneous inputs, seed, fertilizer, pesticide and herbicide, in 1000 rials per hectare of a crop

params.name: pcrop
params.doc: the selling price of a crop commodity, in 1000 rials per ton

params.name: yield
params.doc: the crop yield in metric tons per hectare at each water-stress level along the yield-water curve; the real attribute is model.yield_ because yield is a Python keyword

params.name: water
params.doc: the water requirement in thousands of cubic meters per hectare at each water-stress level along the yield-water curve

params.name: agrol

params.name: luse

params.name: treq
params.index: g x t x c x s
params.doc: the amount of a task required per hectare for a crop grown under a schedule in a given fortnight

params.name: lreq

params.doc: the extra amount of a task required per hectare when cotton is picked mechanically; nonzero only for the relevant tasks

params.doc: the total farm size in hectares available in any fortnight

params.name: lcost
params.doc: the labor cost in 1000 rials per man-day

vars.doc: the area in hectares planted to a crop under a given schedule

vars.name: xwater

vars.doc: the number of units of an equipment item purchased

vars.doc: the labor employed in a fortnight, measured in man-days

vars.doc: the total revenue from crop sales, in 1000 rials

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the cost incurred in each cost classification, in 1000 rials

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: the total farm profit, in 1000 rials

description: For each crop, the quantity sold in tons must match what the farm actually produces of that crop. Production is the area grown at each irrigation level multiplied by the yield that irrigation level gives, summed over all the irrigation levels on the crop's yield-water curve. From that production subtract any product lost to mechanical picking, where the loss is the per-hectare loss rate times the total mechanically picked area for that crop across all fortnights. Set the sales of the crop equal to this net production.
expected_pyomo:
def cbal_rule(model, c):
    loss_term = model.loss[c] * sum(
        model.task['harvest-c', t, 'cotton-p', 'self-prop']
        for t in model.t
        if ('harvest-c', t) in model.taskposs
        and ('harvest-c', t, 'cotton-p', 'self-prop') in model.taskset)
    return model.sales[c] == sum(
        model.yield_[c, w] * model.xwater[c, w]
        for w in model.w if (c, w) in model.xwaterset) - loss_term
model.cbal = Constraint(model.c, rule=cbal_rule)

description: For every task and fortnight where the task is allowed, the amount of work the cropping plan demands must be exactly covered by the work actually performed. The demand is the per-hectare task requirement of each feasible crop and schedule combination times its planted area, summed up. The work performed is the task activity carried out with each implement and power source, each converted to the same units by its technology coefficient. When cotton is picked mechanically the conversion needs an extra adjustment, so reduce the work performed by the per-hectare task adjustment times the mechanically picked cotton area in that fortnight. Require that the demand equals the adjusted work performed.
expected_pyomo:
def tbal_rule(model, g, t):
    if (g, t) not in model.taskposs:
        return Constraint.Skip
    left = sum(model.treq[g, t, c, s] * model.xcrop[c, s] for (c, s) in model.cposs)
    right = sum(
        model.tech[g, m, n] * model.task[g, t, m, n]
        for m in model.mn for n in model.mn
        if (g, t, m, n) in model.taskset)
    adj_term = model.tadj[g] * model.task['harvest-c', t, 'cotton-p', 'self-prop'] \
        if ('harvest-c', t, 'cotton-p', 'self-prop') in model.taskset else 0
    return left == right - adj_term
model.tbal = Constraint(model.g, model.t, rule=tbal_rule)

description: For each crop, the land devoted to it must be consistent whether you count it by schedule or by irrigation level. Add up the area planted to that crop across all of its feasible schedules, and separately add up the area of that crop grown across all the irrigation levels on its yield-water curve. Require these two totals to be equal.
expected_pyomo:
def cropd_rule(model, c):
    left = sum(model.xcrop[c, s] for (cc, s) in model.cposs if cc == c)
    right = sum(model.xwater[c, w] for w in model.w if (c, w) in model.xwaterset)
    return left == right
model.cropd = Constraint(model.c, rule=cropd_rule)

description: The farm's total annual water requirement must equal the water actually used by the crops. For each crop and irrigation level on the yield-water curve, the water used is the per-hectare water requirement times the area grown there. Sum this over all crops and irrigation levels, convert it to millions of cubic meters by dividing by one thousand, and set the annual water requirement equal to that total.

description: In every fortnight the labor needed cannot exceed the labor hired. The labor needed has two parts: the field-work labor, which is the per-hectare labor requirement of each feasible crop and schedule times its planted area; and the equipment-operation labor, which is the task activity carried out in that fortnight converted to hours by its technology coefficient. Hired labor is measured in man-days, so convert it to hours by multiplying by the hours in a man-day. Require the total labor needed to stay within the hired labor for that fortnight.
expected_pyomo:
def labor_rule(model, t):
    crop_labor = sum(model.lreq[c, s, t] * model.xcrop[c, s] for (c, s) in model.cposs)
    task_labor = sum(
        model.tech[g, m, n] * model.task[g, t, m, n]
        for (g, tt) in model.taskposs if tt == t
        for m in model.mn for n in model.mn
        if (g, t, m, n) in model.taskset)
    return crop_labor + task_labor <= model.hrtoday * model.emply[t]
model.labor = Constraint(model.t, rule=labor_rule)

description: The farm's revenue must equal the value of everything it sells. For each crop, multiply the quantity sold by its selling price, sum this over all crops, and set the revenue equal to that total.

model_narrative: We run a farm that chooses how to allocate its land across several crops, each of which can be grown under different cropping schedules and at different irrigation levels. The goal is to make the farm's profit as large as possible, where profit is the revenue from crop sales minus the total of all the farm's costs.

description: The wage cost must equal the labor cost rate times the total labor hired over the whole year. Add up the labor hired across every fortnight, multiply by the cost per man-day, and set the wage cost equal to that product.

model_narrative: We run a farm that chooses how to allocate its land across several crops, each of which can be grown under different cropping schedules and at different irrigation levels. We also decide how much of each field task to carry out with each combination of implement and power source in each fortnight of the year, how much equipment to buy, how much labor to hire in each fortnight, and how much of each crop to sell. The goal is to make the farm's profit as large as possible, where profit is the revenue from crop sales minus the total of all the farm's costs.
